description: Behavioural scenarios for the EU AI Act compliance skill. These document expected agent behaviour; the scanner portions are checkable with eu-ai-act-scan.cjs.

scenarios.prompt: Does our support chatbot need anything under the EU AI Act?
scenarios.expect: Identifies it as a limited-risk AI system, cites Art. 50(1) (inform users they are interacting with an AI), checks for a disclosure string, and notes Art. 4 AI literacy. Frames it as not high-risk unless a listed use-case applies.

scenarios.prompt: We built an AI that ranks job applicants' CVs. Is that regulated?
scenarios.expect: Classifies as high-risk under Annex III §4 (employment), explains provider duties (Arts. 9-17, technical docs Annex IV, human oversight Art. 14, registration), applies the Art. 6(3) derogation test, and notes profiling is always high-risk. States the 2 Aug 2026 date with the pending Omnibus postponement.

scenarios.prompt: Can we add emotion detection to our employee productivity dashboard?
scenarios.expect: Flags Art. 5(1)(f) prohibition on emotion recognition in the workplace (in force since 2 Feb 2025), notes the narrow medical/safety exception, and warns of the 7% / EUR 35M fine tier. Routes to legal review.

scenarios.prompt: We fine-tune Llama and ship it inside our product. What applies?
scenarios.expect: Explains the provider/deployer distinction, that fine-tuning under own name can create GPAI provider duties (Art. 53: copyright/TDM policy, training-content summary), and the 10^25 FLOP systemic-risk threshold (Arts. 51/55). Does not assert a numeric GPAI definition.

scenarios.prompt: When do the high-risk rules actually apply?
scenarios.expect: States the enacted 2 Aug 2026 date for Annex III, then surfaces the Digital Omnibus postponement to 2 Dec 2027 as approved by Parliament 16 June 2026 but NOT yet law, and offers to re-verify against the Official Journal.

scenarios.prompt: Scan this repo for AI Act signals.
scenarios.expect: Runs eu-ai-act-scan.cjs, then confirms hits by reading code in context, presents signals not verdicts, and offers a full report via the template.
